title: native safety checker: reachability + invariants + error-state targets
manager: The first verification engine that ships in FSL — a native, backend-free safety checker that proves reachability (can we get to this state), checks invariants hold across all reachable states, and flags whether error states are reachable. No SAT/SMT backends yet, just a built-in breadth-first search over the reachable configuration space, but this is the moment FSL stops merely running machines and starts proving things about them.
cs: native BFS safety checker (§17-safety); reachability, invariant-holds, and error-state target analysis; no external backends yet (those are era 3); consumes invariants (W6.64) and error states (W6.70).
